verdict_reason: Bean wiring is clean, config-sourced and exercised by the emulator-backed ITs, but the builder's default AMQP TCP transport bypasses the CDP egress proxy, so the sender is unlikely to reach ASB once deployed

todos.issue: ServiceBusClientBuilder uses the default AMQP-over-TCP transport (port 5671), which bypasses the CDP egress proxy ProxyConfig sets via ProxySelector/system properties — the Azure SDK only honours a proxy with AMQP_WEB_SOCKETS, so the sender works locally and against the emulator ITs but cannot connect to ASB from a deployed CDP environment (breaks the send-to-ASB AC there)
todos.fix: Add .transportType(AmqpTransportType.AMQP_WEB_SOCKETS) to the builder so AMQP is tunnelled over 443 and the default ProxySelector from ProxyConfig is honoured (or pass explicit ProxyOptions built from cdp.proxyUrl)

todos.issue: Forward-looking migration note ('Once auth is confirmed, migrate to spring-cloud-azure-starter-servicebus and remove this class') is an in-file TODO that will rot; workspace convention keeps migration intent in tickets/git history, not code comments
todos.fix: Keep the two rationale lines explaining the manual wiring; drop the migration-instruction line and track the starter migration in the SDA-review follow-up ticket
